videos.title: Bakar Napolitansk Pizza På Balkongen
videos.summary_source_text:
Bakar Napolitansk Pizza På Balkongen Vincent Cavaleri gör återbesök på kanalen för att återigen guida oss i hur man gör en perfekt pizzadeg, och hur man ganska enkelt kan twista till en klassisk margarita på två sätt. Alla recept från filmen finner du här nedan och på Vincents hemsida.

MER OM FORS KAN DU LÄSA HÄR*
https://justdrinks.se/varumarken/fors/

RECEPT PÅ VINCENTS PIZZADEG
https://vincentcavaleri.se/recept/#elementor-toc__heading-anchor-6

VINCENT PÅ INSTAGRAM
https://www.instagram.com/vincentcavaleri.se

BAKMASKINEN KOMMER FRÅN OONI OCH FINNS HÄR*
https://www.bagarenochkocken.se/p/ooni-halo-pro-koksassistent-morkgra_67321/ 

San Marzano-reduktion
Mixa San Marzano-tomater och bred ut dem i ett tunt lager på en plåt eller i en ugnsform. Tillaga på 100 °C varmluft tills tomaten når en innertemperatur på 80 °C. Rör om då och då tills reduktionen har en konsistens som tomatpuré.

Pecorinoflarn
Riv 100 g pecorino fint och fördela i 10 jämna högar på en plåt med bakplåtspapper eller silikonmatta. Platta ut varje hög till en tunn cirkel. Baka på 170 °C varmluft i 6–8 minuter, tills flarnen är gyllene. Låt svalna helt innan de lyfts från plåten.

San Marzano-pulver
- Mixa tomaterna släta.
- Koka på låg värme tills de är lika tjocka som tomatpuré.
- Bred ut tunt (1–2 mm) på bakplåtspapper.
- Torka i ugnen på 70 °C varmluft i - - 10 timmar, tills den är helt torr och spröd.
- Låt svalna.
- Mixa till ett fint pulver i kaffekvarn, kryddkvarn eller liten mixer 
7. Om pulvret känns fuktigt – torka det igen på 50–60 °C i 30–60 minuter och mixa en gång till.

Basilikaolja
* 100 g färsk basilika
* 200 ml neutral olja

- Koka upp vatten och förbered ett isbad.
- Blanchera basilikan i 10 sekunder.
- Kyl direkt i isbad och krama ur allt vatten.
- Mixa basilikan med oljan i 2–3 minuter tills den blir varm (ca 60–70 °C).
- Sila genom kaffefilter eller silduk utan att pressa.
- Förvara i kyl med ett tunt lager olja för att behålla färgen

RECEPT PÅ DRINKAR JAG HAR BLANDAT FINNER DU HÄR*
https://justdrinks.se/drinkar/ 

DAVID`S INSTAGRAM
https://www.instagram.com/davidkringlund/

*AVSNITTEN KAN INNEHÅLLA "BETALD MARKNADSFÖRING"
I mina avsnitt så förekommer produkter och varumärken som jag tidigare har eller aktivt arbetar med mot ersättning. Jag väljer alltid själv vilka varumärken jag samarbetar med.

FOTO, FILM OCH REDIGERING
Målbilden med kanalen är bland annat att hålla högsta möjliga produktionsvärde. För att säkerställa det så jobbar jag och Kim Sjöberg parallellt med produktionerna, där Kim framför allt sköter jobbet med kamerorna samt en hel del redigering.

Vill du jobba med Kim så kan du kolla in hans Instagram via länken här under och även kontakta honom där:

https://www.instagram.com/kimsjoberg/

Skål! Vincent Cavaleri gör återbesök på kanalen för att återigen guida oss i hur man gör en perfekt pizzadeg, och hur man ganska enkelt kan twista till en klassisk margarita på två sätt. Alla recept från filmen finner du här nedan och på Vincents hemsida.

👋 WELCOME — I’M RICK SHIELS

This channel is all about helping you play better golf and enjoy the game more.
I make videos for every type of golfer — beginners, weekend players, improving golfers and low handicaps chasing new personal bests.

Here’s what you’ll find on the channel:

⭐ GOLF COACHING & LESSONS

I share simple, practical and proven golf instruction designed to help you:

• Fix your slice
• Fix your hook
• Hit your driver longer and straighter
• Improve your iron strike
• Add height, control and distance
• Learn how to draw and fade the golf ball
• Improve ball position, alignment and setup
• Build a repeatable, consistent golf swing
• Increase clubhead speed
• Improve swing path and club face control

You’ll also find full swing analysis, beginner advice, drills you can try at the range, and tips for getting better at golf quickly.

⭐ SHORT GAME & PUTTING

Lower your scores with short-game tips that help you:

• Stop duffing and thinning chips
• Improve contact and distance control
• Pitch closer to the hole
• Create more backspin
• Build confidence around the greens
• Read putts better
• Improve pace control and holing-out percentages

Short game is where most golfers drop shots — so I make sure you have everything you need to get better fast.

⭐ GOLF EQUIPMENT REVIEWS (H.I.T)

I test the latest:

• Drivers
• Irons
• Putters
• Wedges
• Golf balls
• Launch monitors
• Golf gadgets
• Budget vs premium clubs

All reviews are completely honest and independent. I use Trackman and GCQuad to give you accurate numbers you can trust.

If you’re trying to find the best golf driver, best irons, most forgiving clubs or value golf equipment — you’re in the right place.

⭐ BREAK 75 & GOLF ENTERTAINMENT

Break 75 has become one of YouTube’s biggest golf series.
You’ll see:

• Full 18-hole course vlogs
• Course management strategies
• Matches against tour players and creators
• Pressure moments, birdie runs and disaster holes
• Some of the world’s best golf courses
• Real, relatable golf

I also play matches and challenges with some of the biggest names in golf, including:

• Bryson DeChambeau
• Peter Finch
• Good Good
• GM Golf
• Bob Does Sports
• BustaJack
• Grant Horvat
• Me and My Golf
• Danny Maude

These help bring you the most entertaining and competitive golf content possible.

⭐ TRAVEL, COURSE VLOGS & LIV CONTENT

I film at some of the most incredible golf destinations around the world, including LIV tournament venues.
Expect:

• Amazing golf courses
• Behind-the-scenes access
• Player interactions
• Travel insights
• Bucket-list destinations
• Worldwide golf adventures

⭐ THE GOAL

To make golf more fun, more accessible and help you become a better, more confident, happier golfer — whether you’re breaking 100 or trying to break par. Be sure to subscribe to LIV Golf here:
https://www.youtube.com/@LIVGolf

videos.title: ETCHED IN HISTORY! Carolina Hurricanes' 2026 Stanley Cup Champs are Officially on the Stanley Cup
videos.summary_source_text:
ETCHED IN HISTORY! Carolina Hurricanes' 2026 Stanley Cup Champs are Officially on the Stanley Cup Over the last two days, there has been some news surrounding the Carolina Hurricanes & the Chicago Wolves. The Hurricanes announced the extension of Aleksi Heimosalmi. The 2025-26 Hurricanes team officially has its name on the Stanley Cup, forever etched in history. Also, the Wolves announced their 2026-27 AHL schedule. On the Friday Edition of Locked On Hurricanes,  Zach begins the show by sharing his thoughts on the 2025-26 Hurricanes team having their names etched on the Stanley Cup. Who made it on? What does this mean for the legacy of the franchise moving forward as two-time champs? Secondly, Zach shares his thoughts on the news of the Hurricanes re-signing Heimosalmi to a one-year, two-way deal. What does it mean not only for the Hurricanes, but for the Wolves as well? Finally, Zach concludes the show by sharing his thoughts on the Wolves' 2026-27 AHL schedule. What stands out? What games should fans circle on their calendars? All of that and more, on the latest edition of Locked On Hurricanes.

videos.title: 09.07.26 - RIFT Presentation
videos.summary_source_text:
09.07.26 - RIFT Presentation RIFT is a modern trading platform that combines automated trading, manual trading, futures trading, and educational tools into one ecosystem.

Designed for both beginners and experienced traders, RIFT aims to simplify access to financial markets while providing users with tools that can help them participate more efficiently.

One of the key features of RIFT is its Auto Trading Engine, which allows users to allocate capital into automated trading strategies designed to operate without the need for constant monitoring. This enables participants to remain active in the markets without spending hours each day analyzing charts or placing trades manually.

For users who prefer direct market participation, RIFT also offers Manual Trading and Futures Trading functionality. This gives traders the flexibility to execute their own strategies, manage positions, and take advantage of market opportunities in real time.

RIFT is built around a non-custodial philosophy, meaning users maintain control of their own funds rather than depositing them into a centralized company account. This approach aligns with the growing demand for greater transparency, security, and user control within the digital asset industry.

Beyond trading, RIFT focuses heavily on education. Through training sessions, community support, and ongoing learning resources, users can develop a deeper understanding of trading, risk management, market psychology, and financial technology.

The platform also incorporates modern technologies such as AI-driven infrastructure, advanced execution systems, copy trading capabilities, and future integrations designed to create a more connected and intelligent trading environment.

Whether you are completely new to trading, looking for automated solutions, interested in futures markets, or seeking to expand your financial knowledge, RIFT aims to provide a complete ecosystem where users can learn, participate, and grow.

Key Features

• Auto Trading Engine
• Manual Trading
• Futures Trading
• Non-Custodial Infrastructure
• AI-Powered Trading Tools
• Copy Trading Features
• Educational Resources & Training
• Community Support
• Advanced Market Access
• Designed for Beginners and Experienced Traders

videos.title: Building AI Agents That Automate My Live Stream
videos.summary_source_text:
Building AI Agents That Automate My Live Stream Join the community 🙌 https://mrc.fm/cmc

I'm going live to show you the AI agent system I built today that turns my live streams into finished, posted content while I just keep talking. On stream I walk through the whole pipeline: agents watch the transcript in real time, two models vote on the best moments (a local model plus Gemini as a second opinion), then it auto cuts the clip, burns on kinetic captions with word by word karaoke, and even detects which companies I mention and drops their logos in automatically. Every clip lands in a review dashboard where I approve, bin or edit the post text before anything goes out, because nothing publishes without a human saying go.This is the honest, unpolished version of building an agent fleet that runs the boring half of being a creator, and I'll show you the code, the dashboard and the clips it made from my last stream. Come hang out and ask me anything.

0:00 Testing Gaia Clipper real time transcription
5:08 Monitoring real time clip generation
11:54 Comparing Gemini and primary picker models
19:48 Resolving audio envelope gate errors Join the community 🙌 https://mrc.fm/cmc

videos.title: Anaheim Ducks Match the Leo Carlsson Offer Sheet. Stanley Cup Engraving DRAMA!
videos.summary_source_text:
Anaheim Ducks Match the Leo Carlsson Offer Sheet. Stanley Cup Engraving DRAMA! Anaheim Ducks match blockbuster offer sheet for Leo Carlsson as salary numbers eclipse franchise history—Carlsson will make more over five years than the Ducks were purchased for in 2005. Did the Flyers’ aggressive pursuit shift the market for young NHL stars, and what does this deal mean for future RFA negotiations involving names like Connor Bedard?

Chris Micieli and Zach Pearson break down the fallout, including sharp contrasts with stars like Logan Cooley, and question how the Philadelphia Flyers’ persistent offer sheets could backfire. The hosts debate the controversy surrounding the Carolina Hurricanes’ Stanley Cup engraving—should owners’ families have a place on hockey’s most sacred trophy? Plus: how locker room culture and leadership, from Ottawa’s Brady Tkachuk to Colorado Avalanche’s core, shape a team’s future. Don’t miss this spirited analysis of offseason NHL drama and its ripple effects across the league.

videos.summary_source_text:

Selected No. 2 overall in the 2023 NHL Draft, Carlsson quickly established himself as one of the NHL's brightest young stars. videos.title: The Secret To Flipping The Puck Over Your Opponent’s Stick
videos.summary_source_text:
The Secret To Flipping The Puck Over Your Opponent’s Stick The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

videos.title: Hockey ladder drills 🏒
videos.summary_source_text: Hockey ladder drills 🏒 Ankle strength is massively important for hockey players. And the speed ladder is a great way to develop strength and stability in the joint. Single leg exercises are great for tib, calf, peroneals. Quick two feet drills are great for coordination and muscle firing rate. And the neuromuscular coordination benefits are massive for young kids. If you don’t have a ladder, make one with tape! Ankle strength is massively important for hockey players. videos.title: Ducks Match the MASSIVE Leo Carlsson Offer Sheet ft. David Pagnotta, Hart Levine & Kevin Kurz
videos.summary_source_text:
Ducks Match the MASSIVE Leo Carlsson Offer Sheet ft. David Pagnotta, Hart Levine & Kevin Kurz On today’s episode of The Sheet, Jeff Marek is joined by David Pagnotta from The Fourth Period and Daily Faceoff to open the show and break down the biggest story in the NHL: the Anaheim Ducks accepting Leo Carlsson’s five-year, $90 million offer sheet ($18M AAV). They discuss Anaheim’s decision, the fallout around the league, what it means for the Ducks moving forward, how the Philadelphia Flyers and Danny Brière approached the situation, and what trades, signings, and storylines could be coming next across the NHL.

Then, Hart Levine from PuckPedia joins Jeff to dive deeper into the financial side of the NHL offseason, including the massive increase in free agency spending, the rising salary cap, new contract structures, and how teams are adapting to the league’s changing economic landscape. Kevin Kurz then joins to break down the Flyers’ side of the Carlsson offer sheet, how the move was received in Philadelphia, how Carlsson would have fit into the organization, and what the Flyers’ next targets could be. To close the show, former Florida Panthers Assistant General Manager Steve Werier joins Jeff to examine both sides of the Ducks and Flyers situation, the strategy behind offer sheets, and which players and teams could be involved in the next wave of NHL offer sheet drama.

#TheSheet #NHL #NHLOffseason #Flyers #FlyersTalk #LetsGoDucks #AnaheimDucks #NHLFreeAgency #Hockey

- What happens next in my journey to build a more efficient golf swing

videos.title: Taking the easy way out? No thanks. 😉 At the end of the day, robots don’t play golf - YOU do.
videos.summary_source_text:
Taking the easy way out? No thanks. 😉 At the end of the day, robots don’t play golf - YOU do. Using humans, imperfect as most of you are, to test golf clubs was a no-brainer for us.

It has been our goal from day 1 at MyGolfSpy to create the best golf club review process on the planet. We thought if we use a range of handicaps, swing speeds, and swing types, provide more data, more detail, and be more analytic than anyone else, we could do just that.

We’ve put hundreds of hours into developing, tweaking, and refining our review process.

Even as time passes, we spend hours on the phone with designers, engineers, performance specialists and club fitters trying to make our process even better year after year.

We’ve heard it time and time again.

Humans are too inconsistent.
Humans are unreliable.
You should be using robots.
Your results are invalid.
Let’s be clear.

When the goal is to determine how a golf club will perform on the golf course, data collected from human testers without question, provides the most reliable insight into actual real-world performance.

If you’re looking for something other than real time, data backed results - believe us, they’re out there.

But if your looking for results that pertain to players like you and swings like yours without the marketing bullshit - look no further. Using humans, imperfect as most of you are, to test golf clubs was a no-brainer for us.

videos.title: Grok 4.5 Is 3x Bigger Than Grok 4.3 — But There's a Catch
videos.summary_source_text:
Grok 4.5 Is 3x Bigger Than Grok 4.3 — But There's a Catch Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Elon Musk says Grok 4.5 performs "close to, perhaps exceeding" Claude Opus. No benchmarks. No third-party testing. No public access. Just a tweet. So I dug through everything xAI has actually confirmed about its new 1.5-trillion-parameter model — and separated what's real from what's marketing.

In this breakdown I cover the confirmed V9 architecture, the 3x parameter jump over Grok 4.3, the Cursor training data and live RL harness, the closed-loop ecosystem xAI is quietly building, and the lock-in and cost risks hiding under the hype. This isn't a hit piece or a highlight reel — it's what the evidence supports, so you know exactly how much weight to put on Musk's claims.

videos.title: OpenAI is FINALLY Releasing Hardware
videos.summary_source_text:
OpenAI is FINALLY Releasing Hardware Mark your calendars for July 15th! 

OpenAI is officially releasing its first-ever hardware product this month. It’s a physical, programmable macro pad built specifically for developers to use with Codex and created in partnership with mechanical keyboard maker @worklouder

Reportedly based on Work Louder’s popular Creator Micro 2, this device sits next to your keyboard to give you tactile control and shortcuts for your AI coding workflows. Instead of repeatedly typing out prompts, you can use dedicated shortcut buttons to start or steer coding tasks, approve code changes, review diffs and debug.

#OpenAI #CodexMicro #Codex #ChatGPT #vibecoding Mark your calendars for July 15th!

videos.title: 1000 miljarder vaskade i elbilssatsningar! Biljättarnas fiaskon. Varför?
videos.summary_source_text:
1000 miljarder vaskade i elbilssatsningar! Biljättarnas fiaskon. Varför? För bara några år sedan lovade biltillverkarna en eldriven framtid i rekordfart. Nu skrotas projekt, fabriker bromsas, modeller läggs ned och miljardbelopp skrivs av.
I veckans avsnitt pratar Alrik och Felix om elbilssatsningarna som inte höll – och varför notan nu räknas i tusen miljarder kronor. 
Varför blev det så här? Hur kunde tekniken bli omodern så snabbt? Och vad säger det om de gamla biljättarnas förmåga att ställa om?
Det här är berättelsen om när bilindustrin försökte köpa sig in i framtiden – men upptäckte att pengar inte räcker om produkterna inte träffar rätt.


#elbilar #bilindustri #varför?därför!  #elbil #bilar #omställning #alriksbilar För bara några år sedan lovade biltillverkarna en eldriven framtid i rekordfart. Nu skrotas projekt, fabriker bromsas, modeller läggs ned och miljardbelopp skrivs av.
I veckans avsnitt pratar Alrik och Felix om elbilssatsningarna som inte höll – och varför notan nu räknas i tusen miljarder kronor. 
Varför blev det så här? Hur kunde tekniken bli omodern så snabbt? Och vad säger det om de gamla biljättarnas förmåga att ställa om?
Det här är berättelsen om när bilindustrin försökte köpa sig in i framtiden – men upptäckte att pengar inte räcker om produkterna inte träffar rätt.


#elbilar #bilindustri #varför?därför!  #elbil #bilar #omställning #alriksbilar

videos.title: My Summer Glucose Tips ☀️
videos.summary_source_text:
My Summer Glucose Tips ☀️ My 5 summer glucose tips:

1. Sorbet is not healthier than ice cream.
Sorbet usually spikes our glucose more than ice cream because ice cream contains more protein and more fat, which slow the absorption of glucose into our bloodstream. It's a pleasure decision, so just pick the one you prefer.

2. Iced lattes are often dessert.
The flavored ones usually come with lots of sweet syrups. If you can, opt for some plain iced coffee or plain iced latte with cow's milk or unsweetened almond milk.

3. Most cocktails are sugar bombs.
They're often packed with liquid sugar, and we often have them on an empty stomach. If you can, have a veggie starter or some nuts before drinking, or opt for some dry white wine instead.

4. Mocktails = fruit juice.
They will spike your glucose. Personally I go for sparkling water with lemon instead.

5. Go for a walk after dinner.
Even just 10min of walking around the block or up and down the stairs can help reduce the glucose spike of your meal. My 5 summer glucose tips:

videos.title: You’re Slicing Your Driver Because of THIS Backswing Mistake
videos.summary_source_text:
You’re Slicing Your Driver Because of THIS Backswing Mistake Tired of teeing up on the right side of the box and aiming way left just to survive your slice? If you want to know how to stop slicing driver and finally hit from the inside, you are looking in the wrong place. In this video, we reveal the ultimate golf slice fix that fixes your driver path instantly by correcting your shoulder tilts at the top of the backswing.

Most amateur golfers spin over the top because their shoulders get too flat at the top of the swing, destroying their posture. This simple driver slice fixed drill can be done right at home in your living room or out on the driving range. We break down the exact backswing rotation mechanics you need to drop the club into the slot, shift your club path in to out, and hit long, straight drives.

What You'll Learn:
Why flat shoulders cause an over-the-top move and a brutal slice.

The "Right Foot Alignment" drill to check your shoulder rotation.

A simple home drill using two golf balls and a wedge to perfect your spine angle and dynamic tilt.

How to use your toe line visually to instantly check if you are tilting correctly.

videos.title: This will make you instantly better. (Lefthanded)
videos.channel: Better & Easier golf with your guide Marcus Edblad
videos.summary_source_text:
This will make you instantly better. (Lefthanded) This angle will really show that the swing is not around our body but instead up in front of the body. 

#golfswing #golf #golftips 

Online lesson are now a possibility on Skillest: https://skillest.com/app/profile/marcus-edblad

0:00 Intro to how to understand the clubs movement to a good finish. 

Let me help you get an effortless golfswing with speed, distance and accuracy. I call it the circle of speed. A good release will help you to a better impact and longer drives and irons without slice and hook. How to swing a golf club need to be adjusted to your skill level and experience. 

Long and straight is the goal for everyone but it takes time. Try to focus on one thing not everything when you practise. Impact, face control and swingpath will always be the most important part to drill. We focus too much on rotation but no good golf shot will be executed just by rotating. 

We also need to understand that many myths in golf are misleading. Everything that you find on the internet isn’t true. Ben Hogan, Sam Snead are two of the legends that are often misunderstood.  To understand golf we need to take it step by step and learn every part of the swing before we move on to the next. It's like a big jigsaw puzzle that we all can figure out. 

A will never call a slice sexy but a high draw is beautiful in a very special way. Some pro's that hit draw: Rory McIroy, Xander Schauffele, Patrick Reed, Sepp Straka, Bryson Dechambeau  and many, many more. 

We shall not keep our head still, only start with the hips, look only at the ball or keep the left or right arm straight. Understand what do do with the club and let your body find the best way possible. 

This is not Rick Shiels, Saguto golf, Danny Maude, Meandmygolf, the Average Golfer, Malaska, Mark Crossfield, Alex Elliot Golf or Paige Spiranac. This is a method that anyone can learn if you give it a chance. 

If you use callaway, ping, titleist, takomo or taylor made clubs doesn’t matter you can learn to hit good golf shots. Your loft or your shaft on the club will not change but you can always get better. 

Find what inspires you. Golf basics are the same for everyone. The pga tour, longdrivers or other good golfers can all help you find better golf shots. Watch what they do instead of listed to what they say. Listen mostly to your body and figure out what you need to do to produce the same results as the pros on TV. Be careful when you watch a golf swing in slow motion. The result isn't always what the golfer is trying to do. Feel and real are seldom the same. 

We can find inspiration in a lot of things. You can find training motivation watching movies like Rocky, Tin Cup or Happy Gilmore and find mental inspiration in legends like Arnold Schwarzenegger, Hulk Hogan, Sylvester Stallone or Mike Tyson. Maybe we sometimes need to hear about crazy achievements from people like David Goggins, Jocko Willink, Ct Fletcher, Jim Rohn or Tony Robbins. 

Mike Malaska, Mike Austin, Mike Dunaway, Pete Cowen and Steve Pratt are some of my favorite golf instructors online. Find the tips that make your golf better. Decide what you want to hit. I teach a draw but many like a fade and nobody likes a hook or slice. 

Your hips or your lag is not the key to golf. Your hands will decide the shot and you can do like Tiger Woods and trust your hands. Teach your hands to hit different shots. Hit big hooks and slices to understand what happens. Only straight is not gonna teach you anything. 

Bryson Dechambeu and Kyle Birkshire can inspire you to hit it longer. Tiger Woods and Phil Mickelson can give you inspiration and motivation to test new things and new golf shots. Linn Grant, Viktor Hovland and Ludvig Aberg can show you the future of golf. 

The Masters, Players Championship, The Players, The Open and US Open can make you love golf even more. See the best in the world solve all the problems we all need to master any golf course. Golf is a craft and you need fantasy, feel and skills to master it.

Questions my videos will answer:

How to swing faster. How to hit longer. How to swing faster. How to hit your irons better. How to be better at anything. How to master the circle of speed. How to get a better short game. How to hit your wedge better. How to release the golf club. How to rotate in golf. Best tips for beginner golfers. How to hit down on the ball. How to hit up on the ball. How to rotate hips in the golf swing. How to hit straight every time. How to stop slicing. How to swing effortlessly. 

#subscribe #golftips #pga  #hurricane 


Filmed with Iphone 15 pro or Iphone 17 This angle will really show that the swing is not around our body but instead up in front of the body. 

#golfswing #golf #golftips 

Online lesson are now a possibility on Skillest: https://skillest.com/app/profile/marcus-edblad

videos.title: Detta kommer göra dig omedelbart bättre…
videos.channel: Bättre & Enklare golf med Marcus Edblad PGA
videos.summary_source_text:
Detta kommer göra dig omedelbart bättre… I denna vinkel blir det mycket tydligt att klubban går uppåt framför kroppen. Det ger omedelbart mer kraft och mycket rakare slag. 

#golf #golftips #golfswing 

0:00 Intro till lär dig den magiska cirkeln från grunden. 

Nu finns det möjlighet att genom appen Skillest göra mig till din personliga tränare. Du hittar mig på denna länk: https://skillest.com/app/profile/marcus-edblad

Låt mig hjälpa dig finna bättre slag, bollträff, riktning och svinghastighet. Oavsett om du slår en wedge, järnklubba eller driver kan du med dina händer lära dig golfslag som du inte trodde var möjliga. Längre och rakare slag kräver alltid träning men något alla kan lära sig.  

Vare sig du fightas med en slice eller en hook så har jag videos som kan guida till bättre golf. Är du nybörjare så kan du börja med min grönt kort eller nybörjarkurs.

Försök ersätta dåliga vanor med nya bättre vanor. Oavsett vilket loft eller skaft din klubba har så är det du som bestämmer vad som ska hända. Oavsett om det står callaway, ping, titleist eller taylor made på klubban så kan du slå bra slag.

Var noga med vad du tittar på. Det finns många duktiga instruktörer på nätet, några av mina favoriter är Mike Malaska, Mike Dunaway, Mike Austin.

Försök fokusera på grunderna i slaget bollträff, bladkontroll och svingspår. Träna med fördel på en sak i taget. Tränar du din svinghastighet måste du tillåta att det går snett och tränar du raka slag kan du inte samtidigt svinga i max fart.

Ha tålamod och låt det ta sin tid, luras inte av genvägar som bara gör att du tappar fokus från det viktiga. Golf kommer alltid vara svårt men vi kan med träning se till att vi blir bättre för varje dag som går.

Titta mycket på golf på TV. Bryson Dechambeu, Dustin Johnson, Rory Mcilroy kan inspirera till längre slag och andra som Tiger Woods, Viktor Hovland, Ludvig Åberg och Phil Mickelson kan ge dig fantasi att skapa nya slag.

Träna styrketräning för hela kroppen för att må bra genom livet och skapa en effektiv maskin. Träna händer, handleder och armar för att skapa en effektiv release och mer svinghastighet.

Du kan finna inspiration i filmer som Happy Gilmore, Tin Cup eller Bagger Vance. Filmer som Rocky kan ge dig motivation att ta dig till gymet. Legender som Arnold Schwarzenegger, Sylvester Stallone eller Hulk Hogan kan få dig att vilja bli starkare och Moe Norman, Arnold Palmer, Ben Hogan, Sam Snead eller Jack Nicklaus kan inspirera dig att bli en bättre golfare. 

Ge aldrig upp i sökandet och träningen för att få bästa bollträff, bladkontroll och svingspår.

Filmat med iPhone 17 och redigerat i iMovie på en Macbook. I denna vinkel blir det mycket tydligt att klubban går uppåt framför kroppen. Det ger omedelbart mer kraft och mycket rakare slag. 

#golf #golftips #golfswing

videos.title: This will make you instantly better. (Righthanded)
videos.channel: Better & Easier golf with your guide Marcus Edblad
videos.summary_source_text:
This will make you instantly better. (Righthanded) This angle will really show that the swing is not around our body but instead up in front of the body. 

#golfswing #golf #golftips 

Online lesson are now a possibility on Skillest: https://skillest.com/app/profile/marcus-edblad

0:00 Intro to how to understand the clubs movement to a good finish. 

Let me help you get an effortless golfswing with speed, distance and accuracy. I call it the circle of speed. A good release will help you to a better impact and longer drives and irons without slice and hook. How to swing a golf club need to be adjusted to your skill level and experience. 

Long and straight is the goal for everyone but it takes time. Try to focus on one thing not everything when you practise. Impact, face control and swingpath will always be the most important part to drill. We focus too much on rotation but no good golf shot will be executed just by rotating. 

We also need to understand that many myths in golf are misleading. Everything that you find on the internet isn’t true. Ben Hogan, Sam Snead are two of the legends that are often misunderstood.  To understand golf we need to take it step by step and learn every part of the swing before we move on to the next. It's like a big jigsaw puzzle that we all can figure out. 

A will never call a slice sexy but a high draw is beautiful in a very special way. Some pro's that hit draw: Rory McIroy, Xander Schauffele, Patrick Reed, Sepp Straka, Bryson Dechambeau  and many, many more. 

We shall not keep our head still, only start with the hips, look only at the ball or keep the left or right arm straight. Understand what do do with the club and let your body find the best way possible. 

This is not Rick Shiels, Saguto golf, Danny Maude, Meandmygolf, the Average Golfer, Malaska, Mark Crossfield, Alex Elliot Golf or Paige Spiranac. This is a method that anyone can learn if you give it a chance. 

If you use callaway, ping, titleist, takomo or taylor made clubs doesn’t matter you can learn to hit good golf shots. Your loft or your shaft on the club will not change but you can always get better. 

Find what inspires you. Golf basics are the same for everyone. The pga tour, longdrivers or other good golfers can all help you find better golf shots. Watch what they do instead of listed to what they say. Listen mostly to your body and figure out what you need to do to produce the same results as the pros on TV. Be careful when you watch a golf swing in slow motion. The result isn't always what the golfer is trying to do. Feel and real are seldom the same. 

We can find inspiration in a lot of things. You can find training motivation watching movies like Rocky, Tin Cup or Happy Gilmore and find mental inspiration in legends like Arnold Schwarzenegger, Hulk Hogan, Sylvester Stallone or Mike Tyson. Maybe we sometimes need to hear about crazy achievements from people like David Goggins, Jocko Willink, Ct Fletcher, Jim Rohn or Tony Robbins. 

Mike Malaska, Mike Austin, Mike Dunaway, Pete Cowen and Steve Pratt are some of my favorite golf instructors online. Find the tips that make your golf better. Decide what you want to hit. I teach a draw but many like a fade and nobody likes a hook or slice. 

Your hips or your lag is not the key to golf. Your hands will decide the shot and you can do like Tiger Woods and trust your hands. Teach your hands to hit different shots. Hit big hooks and slices to understand what happens. Only straight is not gonna teach you anything. 

Bryson Dechambeu and Kyle Birkshire can inspire you to hit it longer. Tiger Woods and Phil Mickelson can give you inspiration and motivation to test new things and new golf shots. Linn Grant, Viktor Hovland and Ludvig Aberg can show you the future of golf. 

The Masters, Players Championship, The Players, The Open and US Open can make you love golf even more. See the best in the world solve all the problems we all need to master any golf course. Golf is a craft and you need fantasy, feel and skills to master it.

Questions my videos will answer:

How to swing faster. How to hit longer. How to swing faster. How to hit your irons better. How to be better at anything. How to master the circle of speed. How to get a better short game. How to hit your wedge better. How to release the golf club. How to rotate in golf. Best tips for beginner golfers. How to hit down on the ball. How to hit up on the ball. How to rotate hips in the golf swing. How to hit straight every time. How to stop slicing. How to swing effortlessly. 

#subscribe #golftips #pga  #hurricane 


Filmed with Iphone 15 pro or Iphone 17 This angle will really show that the swing is not around our body but instead up in front of the body. 

#golfswing #golf #golftips 

Online lesson are now a possibility on Skillest: https://skillest.com/app/profile/marcus-edblad

videos.title: Stop Bad Contact Around the Greens — Keep Your Weight Steady
videos.summary_source_text:
Stop Bad Contact Around the Greens — Keep Your Weight Steady Click link in bio for lowest price on the Blue Brick. Struggling with chunks and thins around the green? It's not your fault — most players have never been taught how important a steady lower body is for solid contact.

This video covers the two biggest short game contact killers: hooding the face on the backswing and letting your weight shift all over the place through impact.

You'll learn a simple crossed-feet drill that forces your lower body to stay quiet, so you can deliver a steeper angle of attack and pick up way more spin and control.

We used the Blue Brick to set up a steeper takeaway path during the drill.

Like this video if this helped your short game, and check the link in my bio for the Blue Brick.

videos.summary_source_text:
I Wish Someone Told Me About This Golf Swing Move 20 Years Ago In this golf lesson, Eric Cogorno fixes the lower body mistake that gives high handicap golfers thin shots and topped balls.

⏱️ Timestamps:
🏌️ 0:00 - Eric Cogorno: why your lower body controls staying down through impact
⛳ 1:01 - How the trail foot, knee, hip, and upper body chain reaction works

💪 3:01 - Fix it: push off the inside of your trail big toe

If you've tried every upper body fix and still can't stop standing up through impact, the problem is probably starting from the ground. Eric Cogorno breaks down the lower body chain reaction — trail foot, trail knee, lead hip, upper body — that high handicap golfers get wrong and shows exactly how to fix it with one simple sequence. Watch how Adam Scott and Rory McIlroy use their trail knee and lead hip depth to stay down at impact, and how the alignment rod drill gives you the same feel immediately. If you leave this video without trying the trail toe drill, you're leaving consistency on the table.
What You'll Learn:

What You'll Learn:
• Why incorrect trail foot position causes thin shots, topped balls, and standing up through impact
• How the trail foot controls the knee, hip, and entire lower body chain reaction in the golf swing
• The trail toe fix: pushing off the inside of the big toe to keep the lead hip deep at impact
• The trail knee drill for closing the gap and training correct knee path through the ball
• Why lead hip depth is the real key to staying down at impact for high handicap golfers
• The four-step sequence: trail toe, trail knee, lead hip, upper body — layered piece by piece
• How Adam Scott and Rory McIlroy use trail knee and hip depth that most high handicap golfers never learn
• The alignment rod drill for training correct knee path so your lower body stops leaking toward the ball

Bloating, gas, smelly farts, and other digestive symptoms often all have different root causes and different solutions. 

Healthy digestion starts with strong stomach acid. Your stomach acid should have a pH between 1 and 3 to properly break down protein and help control microbes. Low stomach acid is one of the most common causes of gas and bloating.

Most gas is produced by microbes in the large intestine. When these microbes migrate into the small intestine, they can begin fermenting food, leading to SIBO (small intestinal bacterial overgrowth). Bacteria and fungi thrive on sugar and starches, contributing to gut inflammation, pain, bloating, and digestive discomfort. The carnivore diet can be beneficial for people dealing with SIBO.

Betaine hydrochloride can help to restore stomach acid, but it should be avoided if you have a stomach ulcer. Intermittent fasting is a great way to support digestive health while helping reduce bloating and gas.

If your bloating gets worse as the day goes on, stress may be a contributing factor. Long walks, quality sleep, and nasal breathing can help support gut health. L. reuteri also supports gut health while increasing oxytocin, which can help support a healthy stress response.

videos.title: Backswing Basics For More Consistent Golf
videos.summary_source_text: Backswing Basics For More Consistent Golf The difference in spine angle during the backswing between amateur golfers and the pros can be huge. One common pattern that we see among higher handicap golfers gets them stuck over the ball, while pros maintain their spine angles during the backswing to provide great stability and power. I share simple, practical and proven golf instruction designed to help you:

• Fix your slice
• Fix your hook
• Hit your driver longer and straighter
• Improve your iron strike
• Add height, control and distance

• Increase clubhead speed

You’ll also find full swing analysis, beginner advice, drills you can try at the range, and tips for getting better at golf quickly.

⭐ SHORT GAME & PUTTING

Lower your scores with short-game tips that help you:

• Stop duffing and thinning chips
• Improve contact and distance control
• Pitch closer to the hole
• Create more backspin
• Build confidence around the greens
• Read putts better
• Improve pace control and holing-out percentages

Short game is where most golfers drop shots — so I make sure you have everything you need to get better fast.

videos.title: 08.07.26 - RIFT Presentation
videos.summary_source_text:
08.07.26 - RIFT Presentation RIFT is a modern trading platform that combines automated trading, manual trading, futures trading, and educational tools into one ecosystem.

Designed for both beginners and experienced traders, RIFT aims to simplify access to financial markets while providing users with tools that can help them participate more efficiently.

One of the key features of RIFT is its Auto Trading Engine, which allows users to allocate capital into automated trading strategies designed to operate without the need for constant monitoring. This enables participants to remain active in the markets without spending hours each day analyzing charts or placing trades manually.

For users who prefer direct market participation, RIFT also offers Manual Trading and Futures Trading functionality. This gives traders the flexibility to execute their own strategies, manage positions, and take advantage of market opportunities in real time.

RIFT is built around a non-custodial philosophy, meaning users maintain control of their own funds rather than depositing them into a centralized company account. This approach aligns with the growing demand for greater transparency, security, and user control within the digital asset industry.

Beyond trading, RIFT focuses heavily on education. Through training sessions, community support, and ongoing learning resources, users can develop a deeper understanding of trading, risk management, market psychology, and financial technology.

The platform also incorporates modern technologies such as AI-driven infrastructure, advanced execution systems, copy trading capabilities, and future integrations designed to create a more connected and intelligent trading environment.

Whether you are completely new to trading, looking for automated solutions, interested in futures markets, or seeking to expand your financial knowledge, RIFT aims to provide a complete ecosystem where users can learn, participate, and grow.

Key Features

• Auto Trading Engine
• Manual Trading
• Futures Trading
• Non-Custodial Infrastructure
• AI-Powered Trading Tools
• Copy Trading Features
• Educational Resources & Training
• Community Support
• Advanced Market Access
• Designed for Beginners and Experienced Traders

• Community Support
• Advanced Market Access
• Designed for Beginners and Experienced Traders

videos.title: Build an AI Agent That Runs 24/7 With Tank
videos.summary_source_text:
Build an AI Agent That Runs 24/7 With Tank Get Tank - join the community 👉 https://mrc.fm/cmc

Most people run an AI prompt, get an answer and close the tab. But the real magic is an agent that keeps working when you don't: pulling the news into a daily blog draft, watching listings, running scheduled jobs all night while you sleep. In this stream I'll build one live with Tank, a self-hosted AI worker that lives on your own box and never leaves your data behind. We'll wire it to a schedule, hand it a real recurring task, and cover the bit everyone asks about: how to set it, trust it and walk away without babysitting a terminal. If you've ever wanted your own AI employee that just gets on with it, this is the build.

videos.title: What Does Eating "Healthy" Really Looks Like?
videos.summary_source_text:
What Does Eating "Healthy" Really Looks Like? Healthy eating doesn't have to be complicated. Start with real, nutrient-dense foods that are as close to their natural state as possible. Fresh vegetables, quality proteins, and simple ingredients can go a long way. Organic is great when it's an option, but choosing real food is more important than settling for any processed foods.

To learn more about how to book a consultation with me please check out https://aristotleeducation.com

All of my social media and official links: https://linktr.ee/pradipjamnadasmd

Subscribe to the Channel! https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCOtQHehGWtblMp1gZC8Kq3Q?sub_confirmation=1

Remember that my videos are not a substitute for individualized medical care by a qualified physician. Always consult your physicians before making any diet, nutrition, medication, or lifestyle changes.

About Dr. Pradip Jamnadas, MD, MBBS, FACC, FSCAI, FCCP, FACP

The founder and Chief Medical Officer of Aristotle Education and has been practicing medicine in Central Florida for over 35 years. He is also the founder and medical director of Cardiovascular Interventions, PA, where since 1990 he has been repeatedly recognized in local publications as a Top Doctor. Throughout his distinguished career, he has performed thousands of interventional procedures in both hospital and outpatient settings. As a consultant cardiologist with a large diversified inpatient and outpatient practice he is noted for his passions for teaching and illuminating prevention for cardiovascular disease. He is also a clinical assistant professor of medicine at The Florida State University and University of Central Florida. Healthy eating doesn't have to be complicated. videos.title: The Safest Way To Bring The Puck In Close
videos.summary_source_text:
The Safest Way To Bring The Puck In Close The Downhill Skating System is the mechanics-first approach that unlocks effortless, controlled speed on the ice without extra conditioning, pushing or “trying harder.” Inspired by the movement signatures of players like Connor McDavid, Nathan MacKinnon, Cale Makar, Quinn Hughes, and other elite NHL skaters, this system teaches you how to move on the ice with gravity, using your body's own stretch shortening cycles mixed with the most efficient footwork patterns and various skating mechanics.

Most players skate “uphill”: stiff, blocky, pushing hard, creating tension and burning energy like we were always taught in traditional powerskating. Downhill skating flips that model. By using counter rotation, proper joint angles, and natural gravitational momentum, we teach you move faster with less effort.

Inside the Downhill Skating System, players learn to:

1) Unlock the inner spring for better acceleration.
2)Use different footwork patterns to add options, deception and effortlessness. 
3)Proper joint alignment for efficiency and balance instead of creating tension with push strides.

Apply NHL-base mechanics like shin angle, Y-angle, rhythm and cadence. Generate speed using the rocker, with heel pressure and subtle weight transfers.

This system is not what players would learn in traditional, outdated power skating methods. It’s a biomechanics-based operating system designed to match your body type and natural movement patterns.

Whether you’re trying to improve the forward stride, lateral movements, deception, or posture, Downhill Skating reveals the hidden mechanics the NHL’s best rely on, and shows you how to plug them into your game step by step.

If you’re ready to skate faster with less effort, flow through transitions, and move with the smoothness of the world’s best players, the Downhill Skating System is your blueprint to faster player development.

videos.title: What Is The Difference Between A Crossover and Crossunder?
videos.summary_source_text:
What Is The Difference Between A Crossover and Crossunder? If you play hockey, someone has told you to cross over more. It's the most repeated skating cue there is, and it builds the wrong habit.

Here's what the cue misses. When you lift your inside leg up and over the midline, that leg isn't producing anything, it's just repositioning your feet. Driving with your over leg as opposed to your underleg is where you give up your leverage in the mechanic. The motion that feels like effort is the motion doing the least work.

The mechanical advantage lives in the other leg. When Connor McDavid crosses, his outside leg pulls under his body and drives him sideways before the inside leg ever comes over. Generating the movement from your outside leg takes more energy and more effort. Under the body gives you speed and the hip twist allows for a more mechanically efficient crossunder.

This is why McDavid pulls away from players who look like they're skating just as hard. They're crossing over. He's crossing under. Same shape to the eye, a different engine entirely, and it's the reason we teach it with this language to help players better understand the mechanic.

👉  Drills, breakdowns, progressions, and full mechanics playbook (100's of videos) all available in Train 2.0+ here: https://app.train2point0.com/bundles/membership-plus

videos.title: Anyone Can Make Insane Visual Effects Now!
videos.summary_source_text:
Anyone Can Make Insane Visual Effects Now! How to add AI visual effects to your videos without making everything look AI-generated.

Every Friday, my news videos start with some wild AI intro and it's the thing I get asked about the most. This is the full breakdown of how I do it, plus every other AI effect I use to keep my videos fun.

Intros, transitions, background compositing, B-roll, logo reveals, lower thirds, animated infographics, and the animated talking heads trick from the Sam Altman video. I jump between Runway, Seed Dance 2.0, Gemini, Veo 3.1, Remotion, and Codex throughout.

videos.title: ⚽️ Crispy PROTEIN snack in 5 minutes! Gone before the match even kicks off!
videos.summary_source_text:
⚽️ Crispy PROTEIN snack in 5 minutes! Gone before the match even kicks off! ⚽️ Crispy PROTEIN snack in 5 minutes! Gone before the match even kicks off!

Alright — the World Cup is in full swing, so it's the perfect time to make a snack that's actually delicious and good for you.
3/4 cup (100 g) chickpea flour.
3/4 cup (180 ml) water.
1 tsp salt.
Give it a good whisk. You want a medium batter — not too thick, not too runny.
If it's a little loose, just add more chickpea flour, a spoon at a time, until it's right.
Want a flavor boost? Grate in some fresh garlic — totally optional, but trust me, it's so good.
Fresh dill or fresh rosemary.
Bake in a preheated oven at 350°F (180°C) for about 12 mins.
...until the edges are crispy and golden.
And that's it — crunchy, high-protein, guilt-free game-day snacks. Drop a comment and let me know how you'd rate this recipe. See you in the next one!

videos.title: The 2 Minute Sore Throat FIX
videos.summary_source_text:
The 2 Minute Sore Throat FIX How do you get rid of a sore throat in 1–2 minutes without pills, herbs, or medication? This acupressure technique focuses on applying pressure to the back of the neck directly opposite the location of the sore throat.

🌟 Take the 2-minute Health Lever Quiz: https://drbrg.co/4orvtuZ

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

The technique is simple:
• Find the location of the sore throat in the front of the neck
• Locate the matching point on the back of the neck
• Apply firm pressure for 1–2 minutes

This technique is designed to improve circulation, lymphatic flow, and nerve flow to help relieve a sore throat and keep it from coming back.

#sorethroat #acupressure #sorethroatremedy #naturalremedy #throatpain #healthtips #wellness #lymphaticdrainage #winterhealth #home remedies

videos.title: Robertson’s Future, Ducks on the Clock, and Utah Matches ft. David Pagnotta & Michael Babcock Jr.
videos.summary_source_text:
Robertson’s Future, Ducks on the Clock, and Utah Matches ft. David Pagnotta & Michael Babcock Jr. On today’s episode of The Sheet, Jeff Marek is joined by David Pagnotta of The Fourth Period and Daily Faceoff to break down the latest news, rumors, and movement from around the NHL. Jeff and David dive into the ongoing Jason Robertson situation with the Dallas Stars, the latest trade speculation across the league, key restricted free agents filing for arbitration, and what comes next after Leo Carlsson’s $90 million offer sheet with the Philadelphia Flyers as the Anaheim Ducks face a franchise-altering decision. Could more offer sheets be coming? Which NHL teams are still looking to make moves? Then, Michael Babcock Jr. joins the show after being named the new head coach of the Brantford Bulldogs, discussing his move into the OHL, joining the Bulldogs organization under Spencer Hyman and the Hyman family ownership group, building a winning culture, his coaching journey, and what fans can expect from Brantford this season. Jeff wraps up another packed episode covering the biggest stories shaping the NHL offseason.

#TheSheet #NHL #Hockey #NHLRumors #NHLTrades #DallasStars #AnaheimDucks #BrantfordBulldogs

videos.title: GPT-5.6 Sol Explained: Why You Can't Actually Use It Yet
videos.summary_source_text:
GPT-5.6 Sol Explained: Why You Can't Actually Use It Yet Link to our newsletter: https://bitbiased.ai/
Headlines say GPT-5.6 and Claude Fable 5 just launched — so why can't you use either one? One got pulled by its own government days after going live. The other was previewed to ~20 companies, then went quiet on a release date. In this video I separate what these models actually do from what's just a benchmark chart taped onto a marketing claim.

We break down GPT-5.6 Sol's new "max" and "ultra" sub-agent modes, the three-tier pricing (Sol / Terra / Luna), and that unconfirmed 1.5M-token context rumor — plus why both OpenAI and Anthropic got tangled in the same U.S. government review window within three weeks of each other. Every vendor claim here is flagged as a claim until an independent lab verifies it.

Skepticism-first, evidence-over-hype. That's the whole channel.

⏱️ CHAPTERS
00:00 Neither Model Is Actually "Launched"
1:52 GPT-5.6 Sol: What OpenAI Is Actually Claiming
3:52 Pricing and the Access Problem
4:57 Why the Delay? The Government Got Involved
5:56 Enter Fable 5: Launched, Pulled, Relaunched
7:58 Head-to-Head: Where Each One Actually Wins
9:34 Real-World Use Cases: Who Should Actually Care
11:15 What This Actually Means Going Forward

videos.title: Can This ONE Golf Club Replace An Entire Bag? (8 Clubs In 1)
videos.summary_source_text:
Can This ONE Golf Club Replace An Entire Bag? (8 Clubs In 1) Can one adjustable golf club really replace an entire iron set? In this video, Rick puts the latest Q Club through its paces to find out whether this unique design is a genuine game-changer or just another golf gimmick. From different loft settings to on-course performance, we test whether one club really can do it all.

Welcome to H.I.T Golf Reviews – the home of honest, independent and trustworthy golf equipment reviews.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.

Our testing is built around launch monitor data (GCQuad) and real-world shots, but we always explain the numbers in normal-golfer language. No fluff, no hidden agendas – just whether a club will help your game or not.

Whether you’re:
buying your first proper set,
a mid-handicapper looking for more forgiveness, or
a low-handicap player chasing that perfect combo of feel and distance,

H.I.T is here to help you pick the right gear for your swing, not just the newest thing on the shelf.

Why H.I.T exists:
To cut through the hype.
To give golfers the truth.
To save you time, money and a few golf balls.
To make the game a bit easier – and more fun.

videos.title: New Toyota GR GT is a Porsche KILLER!
videos.summary_source_text:
New Toyota GR GT is a Porsche KILLER! Mat’s teamed up with Toyota for a walk around of the upcoming GR GT. He’ll run you through the design, the performance stats, the interior, and all the key things you need to know. He’ll also have a first look at the Lexus LFA Concept. 

Explore new and used car deals with Carwow: https://bit.ly/Change-Your-Car-0807

Mat’s having his first look at the GR GT! 

It’s an all-new sports car that’s due for release in 2027, and Toyota’s invited Mat down for a look around the prototype version. So what have we learned so far? Let’s take a closer look…

In terms of the design, it’s a car where the production process started with the engineers in the wind tunnel, before it was handed over to the designers. As a result, every single vent you see on the outside is real! From the front to the side to the rear, this car is 100% stick of truth certified! What’s more, we believe there to be an active rear wing around the back, although we couldn’t see it in action with this prototype. It’s also covered in GR and GT badges across the exterior, but no Toyota badges. That’s because GR is now an individual sub-brand, so the official name of the car will simply be GR GT - not the Toyota GR GT! 

When you step into the interior, the first thing you’ll notice are the set of carbon fibre bucket seats. These come fitted with red seat belts, and there are red elements throughout the rest of the cabin - a reflection of this car being a GR development. It comes with a sports steering wheel with a flat bottom, which also has a drive mode selector fitted to it. There’s Alcantara across the dash and roof lining, and there’s also physical buttons below the infotainment screen! We haven’t seen the infotainment screen or digital dials in action yet, although they will be shared in due course. 

Under the bonnet will sit a 4-litre twin-turbo V8 hybrid. We know it will produce more than 650hp and 850Nm, although we don’t have final figures yet. Toyota also claim that the GR GT will have a top speed of at least 199mph - so you can guarantee the final car will exceed 200mph! 

But the GR GT isn’t the only car Mat’s gone to see - he’ll also be having a look around the all-new Lexus LFA Concept! But what can he reveal about this all-new car? You’ll need to stick with Mat and see for yourself! Mat’s teamed up with Toyota for a walk around of the upcoming GR GT. In this golf lesson, Eric Cogorno and JT Thomas explain why low and slow destroys your golf takeaway — and how early wrist hinge fixes it.

🏌️ WORK WITH ERIC COGORNO IN PERSON!

⛳ theHanger Training Aid - https://www.hangergolf.com/?sca_ref=944104.HqM9GYcX2I (Code: COGORNO for 15% OFF)

If you've been told to keep the club low and slow your whole golfing life, this video is going to change how you start your golf swing. Eric Cogorno and JT Thomas break down why low and slow causes golfers to move the body and club together — and why that makes everything harder later in the swing. The fix is early wrist hinge in the backswing: getting the club head moving sooner so you can create separation in transition, stay on plane, and hit the ball more solid. Watch how Adam Scott and Nelly Korda use wrist hinge in the golf swing to get the club head above the hip line early — and how the lead hand drill gives you the same feel in minutes. If you leave this video without trying the lead hand drill, you're leaving solid contact on the table.

What You'll Learn:
• Why low and slow in the golf takeaway causes more problems than it solves
• How moving the body and club together early leads to a worse transition and less solid contact
• The correct wrist hinge in the backswing: hands below hip line, club head above
• How Adam Scott and Nelly Korda use early wrist hinge in the golf swing to stay on plane
• The lead hand drill for training wrist hinge in the backswing from day one
• How early wrist hinge gets the club on plane from down the line
• How the body pivot and hand path create width without sacrificing wrist hinge in the golf swing
• Why getting the club head moving sooner leads to better iron compression and more distance

videos.title: Stretching Not Helping? This May Be Why
videos.summary_source_text:
Stretching Not Helping? This May Be Why Stretching is not always the answer to tightness.
If you stretch a tight area and the tightness keeps coming back — or even feels worse afterward — it may not mean you need to stretch harder.
Sometimes tightness is not simply a muscle-length problem. It can be a learned protective pattern.
In this video, I explain why stretching sometimes does not create lasting change, and I guide you through a short somatic exploration to approach tightness with less force and more awareness.
Try my free guided somatic movement session here:
https://taro-iwamoto.thinkific.com/courses/masterclass

Read my new book:
Your Body Is Not Broken: A Gentle Somatic Path to Moving with More Ease, Trust, and Freedom
A short Kindle book for anyone tired of trying to fix their body and wanting a gentler way to begin again.
https://www.amazon.com/dp/B0GX363HMX

If you feel ready for deeper, more structured support, you can learn more about my course here:
https://taroiwamoto.com/thecourse

videos.title: How 115 million Americans Got Chronic Disease Overnight (Without Getting Any Sicker)
videos.summary_source_text:
How 115 million Americans Got Chronic Disease Overnight (Without Getting Any Sicker) The chronic disease epidemic in the U.S. is not caused by what you think. Discover why overdiagnosis is so common and why chronic disease diagnoses continue to rise. 

0:00 The chronic disease epidemic 
1:03 Diabetes, cholesterol, and hypertension diagnosis
1:18 Hypertension diagnosis changes
1:41 Cholesterol guidelines changes 
2:17 Diabetes and prediabetes
2:48 Overdiagnosis controversy
4:34 Does overdiagnosis help? 
5:46 What causes insulin resistance and chronic illness? 
6:18 Type 2 diabetes reversal
6:51 How to lower cholesterol naturally
7:50 How to lower blood pressure

🌟 Take the 2-minute Health Lever Quiz: https://drbrg.co/4w1Osiz

Learn more about the factors that influence healthy blood pressure in this article: https://drbrg.co/4foKfzv

Explore more videos on nutrition, lifestyle, and the root causes of chronic disease:
▶️ https://youtu.be/oEl1YABvfx0
▶️ https://youtu.be/jFVme1N_wVE
▶️ https://youtu.be/Qgwu_-6wy40

Just so you know, my full line of high-quality supplements is available on Amazon — search Dr. Berg Supplements. 

If you’re dealing with chronic disease symptoms, understanding the root cause is critical, but it’s often overlooked. One of the best ways to identify the root cause is to determine when your symptoms began, and what happened just before that.

In 2017, more than 31 million people were diagnosed with hypertension overnight without any actual change in their blood pressure. Instead, the diagnostic definition of high blood pressure changed.

Similar trends have occurred with cholesterol and diabetes. In 1988, an LDL level of 190 was considered normal. By 2001, that threshold had dropped to 130, and statin prescriptions doubled. The classification for high cholesterol dropped again in 2013. 

Prediabetes was also introduced as a diagnostic category, and today more than 115 million Americans have diabetes or prediabetes.

Expanded diagnostic classifications have not translated into longer life expectancy. Chronically elevated insulin may be at the root of many chronic illnesses, and a high-carbohydrate diet can keep insulin levels elevated.

Natural approaches and lifestyle factors are not always addressed when someone receives a chronic disease diagnosis. 

Overdiagnosed book link: https://www.amazon.com/Overdiagnosed-Making-People-Pursuit-Health/dp/0807021997 

DATA:
https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/20356594/  
https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/31036503/ 
https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC11397061/ 
https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6621350/ 
https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC8108907/ 

Dr. Eric Berg, D.C. Bio:
Dr. Berg, age 61, is a chiropractor who specializes in Healthy Ketosis & Intermittent Fasting. He is the Director of Dr. Berg Nutritionals and author of the best-selling book The Healthy Keto Plan. He no longer practices, but focuses on health education through social media.

Follow Me On Social Media:
YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@Drberg/

YouTube Shorts: https://www.youtube.com/@UCpWhiwlOPxOmwQu5xyjtLDw 

Keto Recipes: https://www.youtube.com/@drbergketorecipes

Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/drericberg

Spotify Podcast: 🎧 https://drbrg.co/DrBerg-Spotify

Disclaimer: 
Dr. Eric Berg D.C. received his Doctor of Chiropractic degree from Palmer College of Chiropractic in 1988. His use of “doctor” or “Dr.” in relation to himself solely refers to that degree. Dr. Berg is a licensed chiropractor in Virginia, California, and Louisiana, but he no longer practices chiropractic in any state and does not see patients, so he can focus on educating people as a full-time activity, yet he maintains an active license. This video is for general informational purposes only. It should not be used to self-diagnose, and it is not a substitute for a medical exam, cure, treatment, diagnosis, prescription, or recommendation. It does not create a doctor-patient relationship between Dr. Berg and you. You should not make any change in your health regimen or diet before first consulting a physician and obtaining a medical exam, diagnosis, and recommendation. Always seek the advice of a physician or other qualified health provider with any questions you may have regarding a medical condition.

*Dr. Eric Berg, DC, is not AI-generated. AI-enhanced elements may be used in this video for production purposes only.

videos.title: The Tuck and Turn Move That Fixes Your Over-the-Top Swing
videos.summary_source_text:
The Tuck and Turn Move That Fixes Your Over-the-Top Swing Click link in bio for lowest price on the Blue Brick. Most players tuck their trail elbow in the backswing and let it fly out in the downswing — it's backwards, and it's costing you a straight ball flight.

This video shows the "tuck and turn": keeping the elbow out in the backswing, then tucking it in transition so the shaft shallows out naturally.

After a few reps of this feel, you'll be swinging under the shallowing line on the Blue Brick and hitting straighter shots or a slight draw instead of a slice.

We used the Blue Brick to give instant visual feedback on shaft plane during the drill.

Like this video if you're going to try this, and check the link in my bio for your own Blue Brick.

videos.title: The BEST golf ball to improve your putting 👀
videos.summary_source_text:
The BEST golf ball to improve your putting 👀 Welcome to H.I.T Golf Reviews – the home of honest, independent and trustworthy golf equipment reviews.

If you want straight-talking advice on drivers, irons, wedges, putters, golf balls and golf tech, you’re in the right place. Every review is based on proper testing, launch monitor data and real on-course experience – not marketing slides.

We test all the big names (TaylorMade, Callaway, Ping, Titleist, Mizuno, Cobra and more) plus the direct-to-consumer brands you’re starting to hear about. New vs old, budget vs premium, “forgiving” vs “players” – we put them head-to-head so you can see what actually performs.

Our testing is built around launch monitor data (GCQuad) and real-world shots, but we always explain the numbers in normal-golfer language. No fluff, no hidden agendas – just whether a club will help your game or not.

Whether you’re:
buying your first proper set,
a mid-handicapper looking for more forgiveness, or
a low-handicap player chasing that perfect combo of feel and distance,

H.I.T is here to help you pick the right gear for your swing, not just the newest thing on the shelf.

Why H.I.T exists:
To cut through the hype.
To give golfers the truth.
To save you time, money and a few golf balls.
To make the game a bit easier – and more fun.

👋 WELCOME — I’M RICK SHIELS

This channel is all about helping you play better golf and enjoy the game more.
I make videos for every type of golfer — beginners, weekend players, improving golfers and low handicaps chasing new personal bests.

Here’s what you’ll find on the channel:

⭐ GOLF COACHING & LESSONS

I share simple, practical and proven golf instruction designed to help you:

• Fix your slice
• Fix your hook
• Hit your driver longer and straighter
• Improve your iron strike
• Add height, control and distance
• Learn how to draw and fade the golf ball
• Improve ball position, alignment and setup
• Build a repeatable, consistent golf swing
• Increase clubhead speed
• Improve swing path and club face control

You’ll also find full swing analysis, beginner advice, drills you can try at the range, and tips for getting better at golf quickly.

⭐ SHORT GAME & PUTTING

Lower your scores with short-game tips that help you:

• Stop duffing and thinning chips
• Improve contact and distance control
• Pitch closer to the hole
• Create more backspin
• Build confidence around the greens
• Read putts better
• Improve pace control and holing-out percentages

Short game is where most golfers drop shots — so I make sure you have everything you need to get better fast.

⭐ GOLF EQUIPMENT REVIEWS (H.I.T)

I test the latest:

• Drivers
• Irons
• Putters
• Wedges
• Golf balls
• Launch monitors
• Golf gadgets
• Budget vs premium clubs

All reviews are completely honest and independent. I use Trackman and GCQuad to give you accurate numbers you can trust.

If you’re trying to find the best golf driver, best irons, most forgiving clubs or value golf equipment — you’re in the right place.

⭐ BREAK 75 & GOLF ENTERTAINMENT

Break 75 has become one of YouTube’s biggest golf series.
You’ll see:

• Full 18-hole course vlogs
• Course management strategies
• Matches against tour players and creators
• Pressure moments, birdie runs and disaster holes
• Some of the world’s best golf courses
• Real, relatable golf

I also play matches and challenges with some of the biggest names in golf, including:

• Bryson DeChambeau
• Peter Finch
• Good Good
• GM Golf
• Bob Does Sports
• BustaJack
• Grant Horvat
• Me and My Golf
• Danny Maude

These help bring you the most entertaining and competitive golf content possible.

⭐ TRAVEL, COURSE VLOGS & LIV CONTENT

I film at some of the most incredible golf destinations around the world, including LIV tournament venues.
Expect:

• Amazing golf courses
• Behind-the-scenes access
• Player interactions
• Travel insights
• Bucket-list destinations
• Worldwide golf adventures

⭐ THE GOAL

To make golf more fun, more accessible and help you become a better, more confident, happier golfer — whether you’re breaking 100 or trying to break par. Be sure to subscribe to LIV Golf here:
https://www.youtube.com/@LIVGolf

videos.title: OFFER SHEET CHAOS! Carolina Hurricanes Rumored to be Interested in Simon Edvinsson
videos.summary_source_text:
OFFER SHEET CHAOS! Carolina Hurricanes Rumored to be Interested in Simon Edvinsson During the mega final episode of 32 Thoughts before the summer break, Elliotte Freidman stated that the Carolina Hurricanes were looking to offer sheet a couple of players. One of them as Leo Carlsson, but the one that caught everyone's attention was Detroit Red Wings' Simon Edvinsson. That being said, does it make sense for the Hurricanes to offer sheet for Edvinsson, and would the Red Wings match it? On the Wednesday Edition of Locked On Hurricanes, Zach begins the show by sharing his thoughts on the news of the Hurricanes being interested in Edvinsson. While they are still figure out what to do with Alexander Nikishin, does going after Edvinsson makes sense if nothing works out? Secondly, if the Hurricanes do off sheet hime, how would that work overall with cap space and pick compensation to the Wings? Plus, how would this change the dynamics of the Hurricanes moving forward if this does play out? Finally, Zach concludes the show by shares his thoughts on where the team is at currently. While they have not made many moves, there is a sense that this will be more quieter than usual when it comes to roster constructon that summers past. All of that and more, on the latest edition of Locked On Hurricanes.
